@AndrewDLuecke Trying to find some data to think about this- and this stuck out to me. I think it's interesting that millenials have this expectation of being rich and famous one day too- way more than other gens. Less likely to tear down "sell-outs" if you see yourself one day too. https://t.co/Ey0E34mjuJ

@AndrewDLuecke I think it could be argued reality television, which purports to show "real people"- and then celebs who got famous "being themselves" somehow hacked the system of "selling out". They were successful because of their perceived "real selves" - highly sold but never sold out.
@ABenalt the system works
